# db_migrate

> Dbmate-style async PostgreSQL migration runner. Single-file tool using asyncpg + structlog.

## What it does

Manages PostgreSQL schema migrations using plain SQL files with `-- migrate:up` and `-- migrate:down` markers. Tracks applied versions in a `schema_migrations` table. Each migration runs in a transaction by default. Concurrent runners are serialized via a PostgreSQL advisory lock.

## Commands

- `db_migrate.py` — apply all pending migrations
- `db_migrate.py --dry-run` — show pending migrations without applying them
- `db_migrate.py --status` — show migration status
- `db_migrate.py --rollback [N]` — rollback last N migrations (default: 1)
- `db_migrate.py --baseline` — mark all migrations applied without running them
- `db_migrate.py --create "description"` — create new migration file
- `db_migrate.py --version` — print tool version (embedded `__version__`)

## Migration file format

Filename: `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_description.sql`

```sql
-- migrate:up
CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL);

-- migrate:down
DROP TABLE users;
```

Blocks run in a transaction by default. Disable per block with `-- migrate:up transaction:false` (dbmate-compatible; needed for `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY`). A `transaction:false` block must contain a single statement — multi-statement strings execute in an implicit transaction.

## Configuration (env vars)

- `DATABASE_URL` (required) — PostgreSQL connection string
- `MIGRATIONS_DIR` (default: `db/migrations`) — path to migration files
- `SCHEMA_NAME` (default: `public`) — schema for tracking table

## Dependencies

asyncpg, structlog, python-dotenv

## Optional: llms-full.txt

For the full source code and API reference, see [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt).
